rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the types of their assigned expressions.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, validate that all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the current scope.
Ensure AGREE expressions use valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and that type mismatches are corrected by adjusting declarations or expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Helper declarations in AGREE must specify correct types that match the expression on the right-hand side of the assignment.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing identifiers or types that are not declared or visible in the current AGREE annex scope.
Do not introduce package-qualified names or types that are not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
irrelevant_topics: AADL component type vs implementation scope distinctions
